This template manually adds an [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]] to a page at the location of its invocation. Usage: 
{{anchor|foo}}
With this you can link to [[Page#foo]] to have people following the link arrive at the anchor location. [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

revision.comment: after testing in [[Template:Anchor/sandbox]] and [[Template:Anchor/testcases]], version 2.0 : backward-compatible but will accept up to 10 {{Anchor|One|Two|Three}} anchors (plus new doc)
revision.text:
{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox|{{Template sandbox notice}} }} {{#if:{{{ 1|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 2|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 3|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 4|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 5|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 6|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 7|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 8|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 9|}}}|{{#if:{{{10|}}}|{{#if:{{{11|}}} |
[[Template:Anchor]]: too much anchors
}}}} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} ==Usage== This template inserts one or more [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]]s in a page at the location of its invocation, enabling direct #Links to this location. ; Code : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After ; Gives : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After The template will handle up to 10 anchor names, but could very easily be extended to do twice or more. If you accidentally use more than 10 anchors, the template will output a bold error message to let you know about it instead of silently ignoring your extra anchors. Anchor names shouldn't include characters "#" (hash), "|" (pipe) or "=" (equal). On the other hand, special characters such as " " (space), "," (comma), "&" (ampersand), etc. can be used directly both in anchors and links, because Wikipedia will process them automatically into an URL-acceptable format. ==Linking== After that, you can have links sending directly to the line you marked: * With [[#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from the same page * With [[Article name#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from another page ==Technical notes== * The template is based on * The final HTML page (as outputted by Wikipedia to the reader's browser) will have converted it to instead. * The more backward-compatible is used by Wikipedia's own headings but disabled for editors in the wikicode and thus not used here. {{pp-semi-protected| small=yes | reason=No need for such template to be anon-editable. }} [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates using ParserFunctions|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

This template manually adds an [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]] to a page at the location of its invocation. Usage: 
{{anchor|foo}}
With this you can link to [[Page#foo]] to have people following the link arrive at the anchor location. [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

revision.comment: after testing in [[Template:Anchor/sandbox]] and [[Template:Anchor/testcases]], version 2.0 : backward-compatible but will accept up to 10 {{Anchor|One|Two|Three}} anchors (plus new doc)
revision.text:
{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox|{{Template sandbox notice}} }} {{#if:{{{ 1|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 2|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 3|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 4|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 5|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 6|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 7|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 8|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 9|}}}|{{#if:{{{10|}}}|{{#if:{{{11|}}} |
[[Template:Anchor]]: too much anchors
}}}} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} ==Usage== This template inserts one or more [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]]s in a page at the location of its invocation, enabling direct #Links to this location. ; Code : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After ; Gives : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After The template will handle up to 10 anchor names, but could very easily be extended to do twice or more. If you accidentally use more than 10 anchors, the template will output a bold error message to let you know about it instead of silently ignoring your extra anchors. Anchor names shouldn't include characters "#" (hash), "|" (pipe) or "=" (equal). On the other hand, special characters such as " " (space), "," (comma), "&" (ampersand), etc. can be used directly both in anchors and links, because Wikipedia will process them automatically into an URL-acceptable format. ==Linking== After that, you can have links sending directly to the line you marked: * With [[#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from the same page * With [[Article name#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from another page ==Technical notes== * The template is based on * The final HTML page (as outputted by Wikipedia to the reader's browser) will have converted it to instead. * The more backward-compatible is used by Wikipedia's own headings but disabled for editors in the wikicode and thus not used here. {{pp-semi-protected| small=yes | reason=No need for such template to be anon-editable. }} [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates using ParserFunctions|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

{{anchor|foo}}
With this you can link to [[Page#foo]] to have people following the link arrive at the anchor location. [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

revision.comment: after testing in [[Template:Anchor/sandbox]] and [[Template:Anchor/testcases]], version 2.0 : backward-compatible but will accept up to 10 {{Anchor|One|Two|Three}} anchors (plus new doc)
revision.text:
{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox|{{Template sandbox notice}} }} {{#if:{{{ 1|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 2|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 3|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 4|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 5|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 6|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 7|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 8|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 9|}}}|{{#if:{{{10|}}}|{{#if:{{{11|}}} |
[[Template:Anchor]]: too much anchors
}}}} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} ==Usage== This template inserts one or more [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]]s in a page at the location of its invocation, enabling direct #Links to this location. ; Code : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After ; Gives : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After The template will handle up to 10 anchor names, but could very easily be extended to do twice or more. If you accidentally use more than 10 anchors, the template will output a bold error message to let you know about it instead of silently ignoring your extra anchors. Anchor names shouldn't include characters "#" (hash), "|" (pipe) or "=" (equal). On the other hand, special characters such as " " (space), "," (comma), "&" (ampersand), etc. can be used directly both in anchors and links, because Wikipedia will process them automatically into an URL-acceptable format. ==Linking== After that, you can have links sending directly to the line you marked: * With [[#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from the same page * With [[Article name#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from another page ==Technical notes== * The template is based on * The final HTML page (as outputted by Wikipedia to the reader's browser) will have converted it to instead. * The more backward-compatible is used by Wikipedia's own headings but disabled for editors in the wikicode and thus not used here. {{pp-semi-protected| small=yes | reason=No need for such template to be anon-editable. }} [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates using ParserFunctions|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

{{anchor|foo}}
With this you can link to [[Page#foo]] to have people following the link arrive at the anchor location. [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

revision.comment: after testing in [[Template:Anchor/sandbox]] and [[Template:Anchor/testcases]], version 2.0 : backward-compatible but will accept up to 10 {{Anchor|One|Two|Three}} anchors (plus new doc)
revision.text:
{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox|{{Template sandbox notice}} }} {{#if:{{{ 1|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 2|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 3|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 4|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 5|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 6|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 7|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 8|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 9|}}}|{{#if:{{{10|}}}|{{#if:{{{11|}}} |
[[Template:Anchor]]: too much anchors
}}}} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} ==Usage== This template inserts one or more [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]]s in a page at the location of its invocation, enabling direct #Links to this location. ; Code : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After ; Gives : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After The template will handle up to 10 anchor names, but could very easily be extended to do twice or more. If you accidentally use more than 10 anchors, the template will output a bold error message to let you know about it instead of silently ignoring your extra anchors. Anchor names shouldn't include characters "#" (hash), "|" (pipe) or "=" (equal). On the other hand, special characters such as " " (space), "," (comma), "&" (ampersand), etc. can be used directly both in anchors and links, because Wikipedia will process them automatically into an URL-acceptable format. ==Linking== After that, you can have links sending directly to the line you marked: * With [[#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from the same page * With [[Article name#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from another page ==Technical notes== * The template is based on * The final HTML page (as outputted by Wikipedia to the reader's browser) will have converted it to instead. * The more backward-compatible is used by Wikipedia's own headings but disabled for editors in the wikicode and thus not used here. {{pp-semi-protected| small=yes | reason=No need for such template to be anon-editable. }} [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates using ParserFunctions|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

{{anchor|foo}}
With this you can link to [[Page#foo]] to have people following the link arrive at the anchor location. [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

revision.comment: after testing in [[Template:Anchor/sandbox]] and [[Template:Anchor/testcases]], version 2.0 : backward-compatible but will accept up to 10 {{Anchor|One|Two|Three}} anchors (plus new doc)
revision.text:
{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox|{{Template sandbox notice}} }} {{#if:{{{ 1|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 2|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 3|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 4|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 5|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 6|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 7|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 8|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 9|}}}|{{#if:{{{10|}}}|{{#if:{{{11|}}} |
[[Template:Anchor]]: too much anchors
}}}} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} ==Usage== This template inserts one or more [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]]s in a page at the location of its invocation, enabling direct #Links to this location. ; Code : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After ; Gives : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After The template will handle up to 10 anchor names, but could very easily be extended to do twice or more. If you accidentally use more than 10 anchors, the template will output a bold error message to let you know about it instead of silently ignoring your extra anchors. Anchor names shouldn't include characters "#" (hash), "|" (pipe) or "=" (equal). On the other hand, special characters such as " " (space), "," (comma), "&" (ampersand), etc. can be used directly both in anchors and links, because Wikipedia will process them automatically into an URL-acceptable format. ==Linking== After that, you can have links sending directly to the line you marked: * With [[#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from the same page * With [[Article name#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from another page ==Technical notes== * The template is based on * The final HTML page (as outputted by Wikipedia to the reader's browser) will have converted it to instead. * The more backward-compatible is used by Wikipedia's own headings but disabled for editors in the wikicode and thus not used here. {{pp-semi-protected| small=yes | reason=No need for such template to be anon-editable. }} [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates using ParserFunctions|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

{{anchor|foo}}
With this you can link to [[Page#foo]] to have people following the link arrive at the anchor location. [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

revision.comment: after testing in [[Template:Anchor/sandbox]] and [[Template:Anchor/testcases]], version 2.0 : backward-compatible but will accept up to 10 {{Anchor|One|Two|Three}} anchors (plus new doc)
revision.text:
{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox|{{Template sandbox notice}} }} {{#if:{{{ 1|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 2|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 3|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 4|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 5|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 6|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 7|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 8|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 9|}}}|{{#if:{{{10|}}}|{{#if:{{{11|}}} |
[[Template:Anchor]]: too much anchors
}}}} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} ==Usage== This template inserts one or more [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]]s in a page at the location of its invocation, enabling direct #Links to this location. ; Code : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After ; Gives : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After The template will handle up to 10 anchor names, but could very easily be extended to do twice or more. If you accidentally use more than 10 anchors, the template will output a bold error message to let you know about it instead of silently ignoring your extra anchors. Anchor names shouldn't include characters "#" (hash), "|" (pipe) or "=" (equal). On the other hand, special characters such as " " (space), "," (comma), "&" (ampersand), etc. can be used directly both in anchors and links, because Wikipedia will process them automatically into an URL-acceptable format. ==Linking== After that, you can have links sending directly to the line you marked: * With [[#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from the same page * With [[Article name#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from another page ==Technical notes== * The template is based on * The final HTML page (as outputted by Wikipedia to the reader's browser) will have converted it to instead. * The more backward-compatible is used by Wikipedia's own headings but disabled for editors in the wikicode and thus not used here. {{pp-semi-protected| small=yes | reason=No need for such template to be anon-editable. }} [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates using ParserFunctions|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

This template manually adds an [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]] to a page at the location of its invocation. Usage: 
{{anchor|foo}}
With this you can link to [[Page#foo]] to have people following the link arrive at the anchor location. [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

revision.comment: after testing in [[Template:Anchor/sandbox]] and [[Template:Anchor/testcases]], version 2.0 : backward-compatible but will accept up to 10 {{Anchor|One|Two|Three}} anchors (plus new doc)
revision.text:
{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox|{{Template sandbox notice}} }} {{#if:{{{ 1|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 2|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 3|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 4|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 5|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 6|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 7|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 8|}}}|{{#if:{{{ 9|}}}|{{#if:{{{10|}}}|{{#if:{{{11|}}} |
[[Template:Anchor]]: too much anchors
}}}} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} ==Usage== This template inserts one or more [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]]s in a page at the location of its invocation, enabling direct #Links to this location. ; Code : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After ; Gives : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After The template will handle up to 10 anchor names, but could very easily be extended to do twice or more. If you accidentally use more than 10 anchors, the template will output a bold error message to let you know about it instead of silently ignoring your extra anchors. Anchor names shouldn't include characters "#" (hash), "|" (pipe) or "=" (equal). On the other hand, special characters such as " " (space), "," (comma), "&" (ampersand), etc. can be used directly both in anchors and links, because Wikipedia will process them automatically into an URL-acceptable format. ==Linking== After that, you can have links sending directly to the line you marked: * With [[#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from the same page * With [[Article name#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from another page ==Technical notes== * The template is based on * The final HTML page (as outputted by Wikipedia to the reader's browser) will have converted it to instead. * The more backward-compatible is used by Wikipedia's own headings but disabled for editors in the wikicode and thus not used here. {{pp-semi-protected| small=yes | reason=No need for such template to be anon-editable. }} [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates using ParserFunctions|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]

}}}} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} ==Usage== This template inserts one or more [[HTML element#Links and anchors|HTML anchor]]s in a page at the location of its invocation, enabling direct #Links to this location. ; Code : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After ; Gives : Before{{{{PAGENAME}}|Foo|Bar & Baz|Three}}After The template will handle up to 10 anchor names, but could very easily be extended to do twice or more. If you accidentally use more than 10 anchors, the template will output a bold error message to let you know about it instead of silently ignoring your extra anchors. Anchor names shouldn't include characters "#" (hash), "|" (pipe) or "=" (equal). On the other hand, special characters such as " " (space), "," (comma), "&" (ampersand), etc. can be used directly both in anchors and links, because Wikipedia will process them automatically into an URL-acceptable format. ==Linking== After that, you can have links sending directly to the line you marked: * With [[#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from the same page * With [[Article name#Bar & Baz|link label]] when linking from another page ==Technical notes== * The template is based on * The final HTML page (as outputted by Wikipedia to the reader's browser) will have converted it to instead. * The more backward-compatible is used by Wikipedia's own headings but disabled for editors in the wikicode and thus not used here. {{pp-semi-protected| small=yes | reason=No need for such template to be anon-editable. }} [[Category:Function templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates using ParserFunctions|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:Modèle:Ancre]] [[de:Vorlage:Anker]] [[ru:Шаблон:Anchor]] [[sl:Predloga:Sidro]]
